extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won Stage 13 after sitting on KRISTOFF's wheel through the final 400m, launching simultaneously and winning the bike throw by centimeters.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN deliberately positioned behind KRISTOFF final 400m, launched simultaneously rather than attempting earlier pass, gambling on superior bike throw.
Quick-Step sent GILBERT Philippe attacking after losing sprinter GAVIRIA, showing willingness to gamble on breakaway without dedicated finisher.
FDJ's leadout delivered DEMAR perfectly but Sagan surfed wheels behind rather than organizing own train, exploiting others' tempo work.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: maintaining peak sprint speed post-Alps while carrying 228-point green jersey lead suggests exceptional recovery physiology compared to specialist sprinters
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: lost bike throw despite equal positioning and timing, revealing technical weakness that cost stage wins at Worlds and here
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: neutral: attacking solo for Quick-Step without Gaviria shows team adapting tactics, but caught 350m out suggests insufficient power for tailwind finish
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 1:39 GC lead allows coasting through transition stages, dream scenario of hard confirmation followed by recovery day
